title: ACDP Registry State (v0.1.0)
description: The registry-maintained portion of a context, returned alongside the body on retrieval. In v0.1.0 this contains only the derived 'status' field; 0.3.0 adds the optional 'lifecycle_events' array (RFC-ACDP-0013, promoting the RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.1 reservation). Future versions will add relationships and attestations. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown fields for forward compatibility.

status.description: Derived from supersession queries, the clock, and (0.3.0, on lifecycle-advertising registries) the retraction state of RFC-ACDP-0013 §7. Precedence: retracted > superseded > expired > active. The registry computes and serves this; consumers can verify independently.

lifecycle_events.description: (0.3.0) Append-only lifecycle event history (RFC-ACDP-0013 §4.1): events in registry-accepted order; never removed, reordered, or mutated. Omitted entirely (never an empty array) when no events exist, per the absent-vs-null convention (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1). Emitted only by registries advertising acdp-registry-lifecycle. Pre-0.3.0 libraries preserve it verbatim as an unknown registry-state member (the field name has been reserved since v0.1.0, RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.1).
